notes: Clean merge: 114 nodes / 143 edges in, 114 / 143 out. No dedup, no dropped edges, no recovery needed.
Node count matches the corpus exactly: all 114 .md files on disk are represented, and every node filePath resolves to a real file (0 disk-only, 0 node-only).
All 114 nodes are type 'document' with valid ids (document:<path>); 0 nodes missing an id; 0 malformed ids.
All complexity values are valid (simple 56, moderate 48, complex 10) — no unknown values to remap.
All 143 edges resolve: 0 dangling endpoints, 0 duplicate (source,target,type), 0 self-loops; every edge has weight and direction.
Edge types are corpus-appropriate: related 130, similar_to 10, documents 3. No imports/calls edges — correct for a pure-markdown corpus; their absence was not flagged.

Layering verified: 110 of 130 'related' edges point INTO common/ roots (patterns 24, testing 22, security 21, coding-style 20, hooks 19); common/ files are edge receivers as expected. Remaining 20 'related' edges are intra-language and react->typescript links.
Cross-reference completeness verified against source markdown: all 103 'common/<x>.md' extends-declarations across language files are backed by a 'related' edge. Zero genuine missing edges.

The README's reference to common/coding-style.md is modeled as a 'documents' edge (README is the index), which is the correct type, not 'related'. A 'common/xxx.md' token in README prose is inert placeholder text and correctly maps to no node.
Import-map edge recovery was a no-op (0 recovered from 114 empty entries) — expected for a docs corpus with no code imports.
